intro: Bradleia lucida, also known as the shiny daphne or puawhananga, is a small tree or shrub native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Pentaphyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Bradleia lucida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is often made into a tea or infused in hot water to create a decoction, which can also be combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
